transcript: Ronnie, you apologised for how you played against Barry Hawkins. There was absolutely no need to apologise this afternoon. Wasn't that magnificent? Yeah, well, I just heard what Steve Davies said. He said I shouldn't try and entertain the crowd, but I never have tried to entertain the crowd. I've just always tried to go out there and play snooker the way I know I can. And if the crowd enjoy that, then great. But that's never been my sort of philosophy. You know, I just like to play the game well. And if I don't play it well, then I get quite frustrated. But the start of the match was, it wasn't, you know, there's a few mistakes, but then I got, you know, I cleaned it up a little bit, you know um and i need to do that these days because i can't pot as well as these guys but i can i can make good breaks amongst the balls and you know um make up for it that way so um yeah i played listen it i won the match that's it yeah you certainly did and now an opportunity to become the oldest as well as the youngest winner of the masters just as you did at the UK last month. Yeah, I don't feel that old. I mean, I feel... I know my age is, but I feel young in my mind. I feel young when I'm round the table. I feel a lot younger round the table than I do when I'm playing these young players. They look old, the way they... You know, their brains are quite slow. So for me, I still feel like my brain's pretty quick around the snooker table, which is enough, you know their brains are quite slow so for me um i still feel like my brain's pretty quick around the snooker table which is which is enough you know so um yeah they need to get their act together because i'm going blind i've got a dodgy arm and bad knees they still can't beat me you still got it ladies and gentlemen you'll see the rocket tomorrow in the final hi i'm ronnie o'sullivan and welcome to Eurosport Snooker on YouTube. Click here to subscribe to Eurosport Snooker.
